metadata.summary: Routing table for 22 research methodologies across 5 topic files. Slug to topic file to agent mode lookup, with concrete trigger and verify steps.

content.title: Methodologies Index
content.summary: Routing table mapping 22 research methodology slugs to one of 5 topic files (idea-generation, market-analysis, user-research, business-model-planning, naming-and-domains) and to the corresponding faion-research-agent mode. Use this index as the O(1) entry point: resolve slug, open the topic file, then read the canonical per-folder methodology if depth is needed.

why.p: The 22 methodologies were originally in a single 1522-line methodologies-detail.md. Splitting into 5 focused topic files keeps each under 2000 tokens, so an agent can load only the relevant slice without blowing context. This index is the lookup table that lets a router or orchestrator skip file scans and jump directly to the right topic file and the right agent mode.

list.item: Resolving a slug (e.g. jobs-to-be-done) to its topic file (user-research.md) before reading.
Routing an orchestrator to the correct topic file before spawning a research subagent.
Mapping a methodology slug to the matching faion-research-agent mode (ideas, market, competitors, validate, personas, pains, niche, pricing, names).
Discovering peer methodologies inside the same topic group when planning a multi-step research task.
Sanity-checking that a slug still exists before referencing it from a spec or plan document.
As the source of truth for methodology content — fields here are abridged stubs; open the referenced topic file or canonical per-folder methodology.xml for the full body.
For agent-integration patterns per methodology — those live in each methodology's own folder under pro/research/researcher/ and pro/research/market-researcher/.
For mode routing in orchestrators — the canonical mode table is in ../CLAUDE.md (## Research Modes); this index mirrors it for read-only lookup, not mutation.
For methodologies outside the 22 covered here (e.g. opportunity-solution-trees, audience-segmentation) — those are tracked in their own per-folder methodology.xml files.

how-to.step: Identify the methodology slug the agent needs (input from user, plan, or upstream methodology).
Scan the section tables below for the slug; record the topic file name and the agent mode column.
Open the matching topic file at skills/faion/knowledge/pro/research/researcher/methodologies-detail/<topic>.md (or the canonical per-folder methodology.xml under the same parent).
If the slug is missing from the index, fall back to a glob search (see commands block) and treat the absence as a routing bug to log.
Pass the resolved mode (e.g. mode: validate) to the faion-research-agent invocation.
After the subagent finishes, write the output file under .aidocs/product_docs/ using the canonical filename listed in ../CLAUDE.md (## Output Files).

list.item: idea-generation — 7 Ps framework for startup ideas — mode: ideas
paul-graham-questions — 4 questions to discover opportunities — mode: ideas
pain-point-research — mining daily frustrations — mode: ideas
niche-evaluation — 5-dimension scoring — mode: niche
market-research-tam-sam-som — TAM/SAM/SOM sizing — mode: market
trend-analysis — timing and trend assessment — mode: market
competitor-analysis — competitor mapping and positioning — mode: competitors
competitive-intelligence — feature gap identification — mode: competitors

rule.statement: Use the canonical per-methodology folder (e.g. pro/research/researcher/competitor-analysis/) for full framework and agent-integration content, not this index file.
rule.rationale: This index is abridged; per-folder methodology.xml carries complete framework, templates, prompts, and gotchas.

list.item: user-interviews — interview design and execution — mode: validate
jobs-to-be-done — JTBD framework — mode: personas
persona-building — creating user personas — mode: personas
problem-validation — evidence-based validation — mode: validate
pain-point-mining — Reddit, forums, reviews — mode: pains
customer-interview-framework — interview structure and questions — mode: validate
niche-viability-scoring — 5-criteria weighted scoring — mode: niche
business-model-research — Blue Ocean strategy — mode: niche
value-proposition-design — value proposition canvas — mode: personas
pricing-research — pricing model selection — mode: pricing
project-naming — 7 naming strategies — mode: names
domain-availability — domain and social handle checking — faion-domain-checker-agent

checklist.check: Confirm the requested slug appears in exactly one of the five section tables above.
Record the topic file name (e.g. user-research.md) before opening any file.
Record the agent mode (e.g. validate) before spawning a subagent; if absent, default to the closest match by section.
Verify the topic file path under skills/faion/knowledge/pro/research/researcher/methodologies-detail/ exists before reading.
If the slug is missing, run the fallback glob (see commands) and log the gap before continuing.
Map mode to output filename via ../CLAUDE.md (## Output Files) — never invent a new filename.
When citing the methodology in downstream docs, use the canonical per-folder path, not this index, as the source.

content.prompt:
You are a research router. Inputs: - methodology_slug: <SLUG> - user_goal: <one sentence>
Procedure: 1. Look up <SLUG> in methodologies-index methodology.xml (5 sections). 2. If found, return JSON: {"slug": "...", "topic_file": "...", "mode": "...", "output_file": "..."}. 3. If not found, run `find skills/faion/knowledge/pro/research -type d -name "<SLUG>"` and return the first hit's parent. 4. If still not found, return {"slug": "<SLUG>", "error": "not_in_index"}. 5. Never fabricate slugs, modes, or output filenames — quote the index verbatim. 6. Output filename must come from ../CLAUDE.md (## Output Files), not from imagination.

example.title: Routing jobs-to-be-done
example.description: Agent receives slug jobs-to-be-done, scans the User Research section, finds mode personas, opens user-research.md, then writes to user-personas.md.
example.note: The mode column wins over intuition. JTBD lives under User Research but its mode is personas, not validate.

example.title: Skipping the index
example.description: Agent guesses pricing-research lives in user-research.md and reads the wrong file. Fix: always look up the section table first.

verify.step: Run validate-this-file command; expect exit code 0.
For each slug listed in any plan, confirm it appears in exactly one section above (no duplicates, no missing).
Confirm every section's topic file exists under methodologies-detail/ via list-topic-files command.
Confirm every mode referenced in this file matches a row in ../CLAUDE.md (## Research Modes) — modes: ideas, market, competitors, pricing, niche, personas, pains, validate, names.
Confirm every output filename used downstream matches a row in ../CLAUDE.md (## Output Files).
If a downstream agent reports a missing slug, log it as a routing gap and add a backlog item to extend this index.

content.warning: This index is a lookup, not a spec. Never copy its abridged item text into deliverables — always open the canonical per-folder methodology.xml or the topic file referenced above.
